Our Story

Honoring Freedom.
Building Community.

In 2020, at the height of racial tension following the deaths of George Floyd, Breonna Taylor, and Ahmaud Arbery, South Suburban communities showed up in droves — even amid the COVID-19 pandemic — to express their anger, frustration, and support of the Black community through protests and vigils.

You Matter 2's Founder & CEO, Destiny Watson, saw that moment as a call to action. With Juneteenth gaining national recognition in 2020, Destiny began organizing the inaugural 2021 Homewood-Flossmoor Juneteenth Festival — an effort to bring the community together to celebrate Black joy, learn about the holiday, and highlight Black-owned businesses.

Through the support of sponsors and partners like the Village of Flossmoor, Homewood-Flossmoor High School, and K. Carlton Inc., You Matter 2 was able to fully fund and host the historic, first-ever HF Juneteenth Festival — drawing over 2,000 attendees, 83 Black-owned businesses, and 15 entertainers in year one.

Since then, the celebration has grown to include a Juneteenth Parade, the first Mr. and Miss HF Juneteenth Royal Court, and has become the LARGEST Chicago Southland Juneteenth Festival — a staple in the community that will remain for years to come.

We are grateful to bring an experience that honors our ancestors, celebrates Black culture, pride, and power — creating a space for us, by us. We are making history and proving that how we're viewed is not the reality of who we are.

Parade Sign-Up

March With Us
on June 19th.

The parade is the heartbeat of our Juneteenth celebration. We invite organizations, churches, schools, dance groups, marching bands, drill teams, and community groups to march with us from 10:00 AM – 11:00 AM on Friday, June 19, 2025.

01

Organizations & Nonprofits

Community groups, faith organizations, and civic associations — represent your mission.

02

Schools & Youth Groups

Marching bands, ROTC, drill teams, and student organizations — young leaders welcome.

03

Performers & Artists

Dancers, steppers, drummers, and cultural performers — bring your art to the streets.

04

Floats & Decorated Vehicles

Showcase your organization or business with a float or decorated vehicle.

📸

Get Involved

Volunteer With
You Matter 2.

This event is powered by community. Whether you're helping set up, guiding the parade, keeping the block party fun, or packing it all up at the end — your time makes a real difference.

🧭
Parade Marshal
Guide parade participants and keep the route safe and on schedule during the 10–11 AM march.
🔧
Set Up Crew
Help prepare the venue, stages, tents, and vendor areas before the celebration begins.
🎪
Activities Attendant
Assist with block party activities, games, and keep the energy alive during the 11 AM–2 PM party.
🧹
Breakdown Crew
Help break down, clean up, and restore the venue once the celebration wraps up.
📸

Block Party Vendors

Showcase Your
Business With Us.

We are proud to highlight Black-owned and community businesses at our block party. Whether you sell food, crafts, clothing, services, or goods — this is your chance to put your brand in front of thousands of community members.

🍗

Food & Beverage Vendors

Serve your specialty dishes, drinks, and treats to a hungry, celebratory crowd.

🛍️

Artisan & Retail Vendors

Clothing, art, jewelry, candles, books, and more — bring your shop to the block.

💼

Service & Resource Providers

Health services, nonprofits, financial resources — connect with the community.

🌟

Why Vendor With Us?

We've hosted 83+ Black-owned vendors and thousands of attendees. This is a proven, high-traffic community event.
